Treatment Arm - N.value: ADT plus orteronel arm: 638, control arm: 641

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily)

Trial Name.value: Orteronel for Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: A Multicenter, Randomized, Open-Label Phase III Trial (SWOG-1216)

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Overall survival (OS), which was defined as the time from random assignment to the date of death from any cause.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) response rates (defined as complete response (CR; PSA < 0.2 ng/mL), partial response (PR; PSA between 0.2 and 4.0 ng/mL), and no response (NR; PSA > 4.0 ng/mL) at a 7-month landmark after random assignment), and adverse events (AEs) rate. PFS was specifically defined as the time from random assignment to first documentation of PSA progression (≥ 25% increase and an absolute increase of at least 2 ng/mL from the nadir PSA), radiologic progression (two or more new lesions on radionuclide bone scans, per Prostate Cancer Working Group 2 [PCWG2] criteria, and/or soft tissue progression per RECIST version 1.1), clinical progression (symptomatic deterioration) or death, whichever occurred first.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: Median PFS: 47.6 months, 50.2% at 4 years. Hazard ratio (treatment vs control) for PFS: 0.58 (95% CI, 0.51 to 0.67); P < .0001.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.value: Median PFS: 23.0 months, 33.9% at 4 years. Hazard ratio (treatment vs control) for PFS: 0.58 (95% CI, 0.51 to 0.67); P < .0001.

Type of Therapy.value: Participants were randomly assigned to either androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily).

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.value: The lack of correlation of progression-free survival (PFS) and prostate-specific antigen (PSA) response with overall survival (OS) raises concerns over assumption of their consistent surrogacy for OS in the context of extensive postprotocol therapy in this setting.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: 59.7% at 5 years in the orteronel group

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.value: 57.9% at 5 years in the control group
